summary: High‑energy NYE show with a standout LTGTR > Franklin's Tower opener and strong second set, captured on a well‑remastered matrix mix.
review: The performance is celebrated for its electric first‑set flow (Touch → Sugar Magnolia → Women Are Smarter) and a thunderous second set featuring the rare LTGTR > Franklin's Tower jam, Wang Dang Doodle and an inspired Olatunji drum segment.  The matrix recording – essentially a Jay Ashley/Bertha SBD remaster with Healy mix tweaks – is generally clear and balanced, though a few listeners note occasional drop‑outs or that the FM broadcast version sounds less pristine.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating is 3.75 across 16 reviews, but overwhelming positive language about the performance and recording pushes the AI‑derived rating into the good‑to‑great range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Many reviewers praise the Jay Ashley/Bertha SBD remaster and Healy mix for clarity; a few note it is an FM broadcast capture with minor drop‑outs or less fidelity than dedicated soundboard versions.

poor_songs: Franklin's Tower (one reviewer called it sloppy)
The Dew (runs out of gas in some parts)
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑octane opening trio (Sugar Magnolia → Touch → Women Are Smarter) slows with a beautiful Terrapin Station, then explodes into an Olatunji drum segment and a second set that many cite as the night’s highlight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for screaming guitar on The Dew, extra umph on Franklin's Tower, and engaging stage presence (eye‑contact moments).
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for prominent, dark bass lines during Cassidy and overall solid playing throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s organ work highlighted – ethereal intro to Gimme Some Lovin', soaring lines on West L.A., and tight interplay with Jerry.
band_member_comments.Drums: Olatunji percussion segment described as “fuckin' awesome”; Mickey’s sound‑effects during jams also received kudos.
